text:
There once was a humble ant who lived on a leaf near a river. Every day she’d carry bits of flowers home.

One day, as she was traversing the leafy path home, a small bird swooped down. The bird caught a drop of water droplet mistaking it for nectar.

---

Meanwhile, the ant collected so many petals.

---

After many days, a creature big as a cloud decided to settle nearby. It wanted to drink the river—it smelled just like juice.

---

The cloud drank greedily.

---

Unfortunately, it was a bad drink. It felt bad. Too bad.

---

So it told the ants everything. About how it felt bad drinking all that river.

---

That’s when the ant remembered something she’d been told by her wise old grandmother.

She whispered it into the cloud’s ear:

“Cherish every drop.”

---

So the cloud listened with gratitude.

---

The river flowed more softly.

---

The cloud began to enjoy its new homeby
